what_it_does: This file is a generated surface-specification JSON for view G1, describing a planned responsive Desktop-to-tablet-to-mobile reflow surface for NODE-kinds, listing the UI addresses it binds to and the components, lint command, and token source it builds toward.
observations: It is a surface-spec for view id G1 in area G, status planned, with home_journey set to _unrouted.
The title is 'Desktop→tablet→mobile reflow', platform is responsive, and it represents NODE-kinds.
Both journeys and cross_journeys arrays are empty.
It lists three ui:// addresses: canvas/node, chat/node-palette, and rail/palette/node-kinds.
build_to names components canvas, chat, and rail, a design_lint command, and a tokens-to-CSS mapping.
The _what field records that it was generated by a Claude Code session and instructs not to invent addresses.
standouts: Empty journeys and cross_journeys despite being in a journey-spine directory and described as carrying the web (B3).
The address list is the concrete binding contract to three distinct UI endpoints.
Tokens are declared as the only look-source via design-system.css.
conventions: Follows a blueprint surface-spec schema with id, area, title, platforms, represents, status, home_journey, journeys, cross_journeys, addresses, and build_to.
Uses _unrouted as the home_journey for a surface not yet assigned to a journey.
Breaks its own stated convention that journeys/cross_journeys carry the web (B3), because both arrays are empty.
concerns: Planned status with empty journeys and cross_journeys means the surface is not yet wired into the journey spine.
The design_lint value contains an incomplete command fragment '(the FORM gate)' with no documented semantics.
The 'never invent an address' instruction is meta-commentary inside _what, not a machine-enforceable schema rule.
notes: G1.json is a placeholder-style responsive surface spec for node-kind UI across canvas, chat, and rail, awaiting journey routing.
questions: What does 'B3' refer to in the context of journeys/cross_journeys?
What is the FORM gate and how is the lint command intended to enforce it?
Why is the surface unrouted despite having concrete addresses and build targets?
What does 'NODE-kinds' represent concretely in this design system?
purpose_vs_actual: Partially: it is a surface spec for G1, but its journeys and cross_journeys are empty, so it does not yet fulfill the stated role of carrying the web (B3).
apparent_themes: Separation of look-source (tokens) from component build targets.
Explicit routing contracts via journeys and cross_journeys.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to define a single responsive view (G1) that governs how node-kind UI surfaces across canvas, chat, and rail, while anchoring visual consistency to a centralized token system and a lint gate.
novelty: The self-documenting _what field that records the generating session and a rule against inventing addresses, plus the explicit claim that the token file is the only look-source.
contribution: It contributes a concrete responsive surface definition for node-kind UI with explicit component addresses, build targets, and a declared token source, which a fused system could keep as a routing and build contract once journey wiring is added.
summary_for_embedding: G1.json is a planned responsive surface specification for view G1 covering node-kind UI across canvas, chat, and rail, with empty journey routing, three UI addresses, and build, lint, and token targets.
intention_for_embedding: It was intended to blueprint a desktop-to-mobile reflow surface for NODE-kinds and bind it to specific component addresses, a lint gate, and a single token source.
